case.title: LOUISIANA EDUC. COMM. FOR NEEDY CHILDREN v. POINDEXTER

opinion.h: LOUISIANA EDUC. COMM. FOR NEEDY CHILDREN v. POINDEXTER, 393 U.S. 17 (1968)
LOUISIANA EDUCATION COMMISSION FOR NEEDY CHILDREN ET AL. v. POINDEXTER ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE EASTERN DISTRICT OF LOUISIANA. No. 284. Decided October 14, 1968.
opinion.p: Leander H. Perez and Luke A. Petrovich for appellants.
Solicitor General Griswold and Assistant Attorney General Pollak for appellees.
The motion to affirm is granted and the judgment is affirmed.
opinion.h: WESTSIDE LIQUOR CO. v. KIRBY, 393 U.S. 17 (1968)
opinion.p: 393 U.S. 17 WESTSIDE LIQUOR CO. v. KIRBY, DIRECTOR, DEPARTMENT OF ALCOHOLIC BEVERAGE CONTROL OF CALIFORNIA. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEAL OF CALIFORNIA, SECOND APPELLATE DISTRICT. No. 324. Decided October 14, 1968.
259 Cal. App. 2d 511, 66 Cal. Rptr. 434, appeal dismissed.
Thomas C. Lynch, Attorney General of California, and Lynn Henry Johnson, Deputy Attorney General, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
